key_insight: Explicitly sampling non-ideal configurational entropy, together with vibrational entropy, shifts predicted binary alloy phase boundaries and improves agreement with experiment for Au-Cu.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical atomistic route to build more reliable composition–temperature phase diagrams for alloy design, especially when order–disorder transitions matter.
method: Alchemical thermodynamic integration with composition-dependent transformations
Monte Carlo identity-exchange moves to capture non-ideal configurational entropy
Molecular dynamics to sample vibrational entropy along the free-energy path
evidence_signal: For AuCu, the order–disorder transition temperature drops from about 810 K to 710 K when explicit configurational sampling is included, closer to the experimental 683 K, and the solid-solution stability range widens.
watch_item: Agreement is highly sensitive to the exchange-correlation functional, so good results for one ACE model should not be taken as broadly predictive without careful functional choice.
paper_title: Computing binary alloy phase diagrams with explicit configurational and vibrational entropy
